_comment: TCEL P4.1 — LITERAL starting-point skeleton for a Knowledge Package manifest. Copy this file, rename it, and fill it in. Every section the kernel accepts is present with one fully-populated placeholder element. _comment keys are ignored by the loader (unknown properties are dropped) and exist only to explain each field. Delete the sections you do not use — empty arrays are fine. Before authoring a follow-on or multi-wave package, FIRST consult GET /api/packages/id-ledger and treat every id it lists as reserved.
_comment_identity: Required: packageId, name, packageVersion. packageId is the stable identity; a changed manifest is a NEW packageVersion, never an overwrite.
name: Example Domain Package
_comment_dependencies: Optional. Declare ONLY lower-numbered / already-existing packages (strictly cumulative — cycle-free by construction). The loader REJECTS any manifest that would close a dependency cycle. A forward/loose coupling belongs in prose here, never as a dependency edge.
_comment_supersedes: Optional (P2.1). Earlier drafts THIS package replaces. Declared by the replacing package only; supersededBy is derived, never stored. Superseding a still-active package warns — the operator deactivates it deliberately.
_comment_consolidates: Optional (P2.3). Package ids this one aggregates while they STAY active (distinct from supersedes, which replaces). Consolidating a package that was never loaded is a load ERROR; consolidating one whose ids you never reference is a WARNING — populate the mapping.
_comment_entities: Domain record/entity types this package contributes.
_comment_events: Kernel events this package declares. Rules trigger on these (or on events an active dependency declares).
_comment_rules: Deterministic rules. A rule must do something: an OutcomeOnMatch, EmitScores, or both. riskType tags ride KPI/score emissions, not a bespoke risk entity.

rules.name: Flag large example amounts
rules.description: System behavior: amounts over the threshold are flagged for review.

capabilities.name: Example Intelligence
capabilities.description: What this package does, in one line.

_comment_schemaTemplates: Declarative field mappings from a raw source shape to a target entity. sourceFormat is json | csv | text.
_comment_performanceFrameworks: KPI/OKR frameworks. A KPI formula supports + - * / and parentheses over payload fields; riskType tags a KPI as a risk-register entry. subjectType may be OrganizationalUnit, User, or any entity type.

performanceFrameworks.name: Example KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Boundary vs <neighbor-packageId>: state here what this framework deliberately does NOT cover when a neighbor already exists.

_comment_agents: Agent spec — see agent-template.json for the full Identity block. A missing owner is a load WARNING. Before adding an agent, check the tenant's active agents (and GET /api/packages/id-ledger) for semantic overlap; the loader warns on overlapping scope.

agents.name: Example Guide
agents.description: One-line scope. Boundary vs <neighbor-agentId>: what it deliberately does not do.
agents.persona: You are ... You ground every answer in the provided context and never invent facts.
sampleQuestions: What example records need review right now?
prohibited: never state a determination the tenant's licensed professional must make
agents.outputDisclaimer: Guidance only — decisions are made by a qualified human, not this agent.

_comment_knowledgeAssets: Effective-dated reference content, optionally with knowledge-graph relationship edges.
_comment_aiExtractionTemplates: AI-driven extraction from unstructured documents, with a per-field confidence review threshold in (0,1].
